title: DAM-VLA: Decoupled Asynchronous Multimodal Vision-Language-Action Model
summary: Vision-language-action (VLA) models inherit a shared synchronous clock from vision-language pretraining, processing every input at one rate — a fundamental mismatch since physical sensors operate at different frequencies. DAM-VLA addresses this by maintaining separate latent buffers for each modality that update at their respective sensor rates, using gated cross-attention to integrate high-frequency inputs while preserving the pretrained foundation model. The approach achieves a 95.2% average success rate on seven contact-rich real-world manipulation tasks, more than doubling the strongest baseline (40.95%), while maintaining a responsive 100 Hz control frequency.
